title: Logging templates should be constant
description: Logging arguments should not require evaluation in order to avoid unnecessary performance overhead. When passing concatenated strings or string interpolations directly into a logging method, the evaluation of these expressions occurs every time the logging method is called, regardless of the log level. This can lead to inefficient code execution and increased resource consumption.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.triggering_conditions: Logging method with string concatenation (+) or string interpolation ($"") instead of constant template
never_trigger_on: Structured logging with constant templates and parameters
logic.reasoning: Detects logging calls using string concatenation or interpolation which causes unnecessary evaluation overhead. Matches logging methods followed by either variable + concatenation or $" interpolation. Excludes test contexts and ToString methods where performance is not critical.
